audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Sardis, Philadelphia, Laodicea, open door, lukewarm warning, beloved discipline, throne promise.

verses.restored: To the messenger of the assembly in Sardis write: These things says the One having the seven Spirits of the Divine and seven stars. I know your works, that you have name that you live, and you are dead.
Become awake and strengthen the remaining things that were about to die, for I have not found your works completed before my Divine.
Remember therefore how you received and heard; keep it and turn/realign. If you do not wake, I will come like thief, and you will not know what hour I come upon you.
But you have a few names in Sardis who did not soil their garments; they will walk with me in white, because they are worthy.
The one conquering will be clothed thus in white garments, and I will not wipe one's name from scroll of life, and I will confess that name before my Father and his messengers.
The one having ear, hear what Spirit says to the assemblies.
To the messenger of the assembly in Philadelphia write: These things says the Holy One, the True One, having key of David, opening and no one will shut, shutting and no one opens.
I know your works. Look, I have given before you an opened door that no one is able to shut, because you have little power and kept my word and did not deny my name.
Look, I give from the assembly of the adversarial one, those saying they are Judeans and are not but lie; look, I will make them come and bow before your feet and know that I loved you.
Because you kept the word of my endurance, I also will keep you from the hour of testing about to come upon the whole inhabited world, to test those dwelling upon earth.
I come quickly. Hold what you have, so that no one takes your crown.

The one conquering, I will make that one pillar in the temple of my Divine, and one will never go out; and I will write upon that one the name of my Divine, and name of city of my Divine, the new Jerusalem coming down from heaven, and my new name.
The one having ear, hear what Spirit says to the assemblies.
To the messenger of the assembly in Laodicea write: These things says the Amen, the trustworthy and true witness, the beginning/source of the creation of the Divine.
I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot. I wish you were cold or hot.
So, because you are lukewarm and neither hot nor cold, I am about to spit you from my mouth.
Because you say, I am rich and have prospered and need nothing, and you do not know that you are wretched and pitiable and poor and blind and naked,

I counsel you to buy from me gold refined by fire so you may be rich, and white garments so you may be clothed and shame of your nakedness not manifest, and eye-salve to anoint your eyes so you may see.
As many as I love, I expose and discipline; therefore be zealous and turn/realign.
Look, I stand at the door and knock; if anyone hears my voice and opens the door, I will enter to that one and eat with that one and that one with me.
The one conquering, I will give to sit with me on my throne, as I also conquered and sat with my Father on his throne.
The one having ear, hear what Spirit says to the assemblies.

translation_notes: Philadelphia's 'Judeans and are not' language is local conflict rhetoric and must not become anti-Jewish theology.
Laodicea is critiqued for self-sufficient wealth blindness; invitation is table fellowship.
Discipline language is love/exposure/turning, not abuse.
safety_note: Do not weaponize assembly diagnoses against modern churches or people; they are symbolic pastoral calls to wake, endure, open, and turn.
